title: GWI POS Install Event
description: Schema for a single line in the append-only install-events.jsonl log. Each line is one independent JSON object. Written by the Stage 11 wrapper for every baseline execution (success or failure), lock events, queue pruning, and other significant lifecycle events. Lives at /opt/gwi-pos/state/install-events.jsonl. Each failed or skipped baseline run MUST produce an entry per Rule 11. Example line: {"schema_version":"1.0","producer":"11-system-hardening.sh","generated_at":"2026-03-20T14:35:00Z","node_id":"nuc-venue-001","baseline_version":"baseline-2026.03.20.1","event":"baseline_run_complete","triggered_by":"installer","details":{"outcome":"success","duration_seconds":142,"changed_count":3},"outcome":"success"}

producer.description: Name of the script or service that wrote this event.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when this event occurred.

node_id.description: Unique node identifier from SERVER_NODE_ID in .env.

event.description: Event type identifier. Known events: baseline_run_start, baseline_run_complete, baseline_run_failed, lock_acquired, lock_released, lock_force_unlocked, reboot_scheduled, reboot_deferred, verify_complete, queue_pruned, bootstrap_policy_created, snapshot_created.

details.description: Event-specific details. Structure varies by event type. For baseline_run_complete: outcome, duration_seconds, changed_count. For queue_pruned: count, reason. For lock events: pid, holder.

outcome.description: High-level outcome of the event. For baseline runs: success, success_with_warnings, failed_required, skipped_unavailable. For other events: completed, deferred, error.
